_meta.description: Hand-curated prerequisite edges against the ASSISTments 2012-2013 skill taxonomy, used by Phase 2 PR A3 to validate the concept graph builder in ml/simulator/data/concept_graph.py.
_meta.source: Edges derived from standard US elementary-through-algebra-1 math curriculum progressions (CCSSM 6-8 + Algebra 1). Each edge is defensible on pedagogical grounds: the parent concept is operationally required to solve the child concept's problems. Where an ASSISTments skill name appears at multiple skill_id values (the 2012-2013 release re-tags some skills across versions), we pick the id with the most responses in the loaded (min_responses_per_item=150) slice.
_meta.format: Each edge is {prereq_id, successor_id, prereq_name, successor_name, rationale}. Edges are directed: prereq -> successor means 'prereq is required before successor'.
_meta.skill_taxonomy_source: ASSISTments 2012-2013 'skill' / 'skill_id' columns (the release's own curriculum tags)
_meta.excludes_same_name_edges: e.g. skill_id 48 vs 588 (both 'Equivalent Fractions') are separate release tags for the same concept; we treat them as duplicates and use 48.

edges.rationale: Subtraction is defined as the inverse of addition.
Multiplication is repeated addition.
Long multiplication uses place-value subtraction.
Division is the inverse of multiplication.
Decimal multiplication is whole-number multiplication with place-value bookkeeping.
Fraction division reduces to whole-number division after inverting the divisor.
Fraction addition requires a common denominator found via equivalent fractions.
Fractions are ordered by expressing them with a common denominator.
Mixed-number addition decomposes into whole-number + proper-fraction addition.
Mixed-number subtraction decomposes into whole-number + proper-fraction subtraction.
Mixed-number multiplication converts mixed to improper, then proper-fraction multiplication.
Mixed-number division converts mixed to improper, then proper-fraction division.
Fraction multiplication uses whole-number multiplication on numerators and denominators.
Integer ordering extends whole-number ordering across the sign.
Real-number ordering extends integer ordering by density.
Real-number ordering subsumes fraction ordering.
Reducing a fraction to simplest form uses the GCF of numerator and denominator.
GCF is the product of shared prime factors.
Prime factorisation presupposes the concept of a prime.
Area formulas build on length measurement established by perimeter.
A parallelogram has the same area as a rectangle with matching base and height (shearing argument).
A triangle is half a rectangle sharing its base and height.
A trapezoid decomposes into a parallelogram plus a triangle, or into two triangles averaged.
Circumference presupposes the definition of a circle (centre + radius).
Circumference formula C = 2 * pi * r uses the constant pi by definition.
Area formula A = pi * r^2 can be derived by 'unrolling' the circumference into a triangle.
Volume of a prism is base area (rectangle area) times height.
Surface area builds on understanding the prism as a 3D object (introduced via volume).
Full order of operations extends the positive-reals case with negatives and exponents.

edges.successor_name: Equation Solving Two or Fewer Steps
edges.rationale: Two-step equation solving often requires combining like terms on one side first.

edges.prereq_name: Equation Solving Two or Fewer Steps
edges.successor_name: Equation Solving More Than Two Steps
edges.rationale: Multi-step solving chains multiple two-step inverses.
Distributing a(b + c) is a prerequisite to identifying like terms produced by the distribution.
Solving an algebraic equation typically requires first simplifying each side.
The distance formula is the Pythagorean theorem applied to coordinate differences.
Scientific notation expresses numbers as a mantissa times a power of ten (an exponent).
Factoring-based quadratic solving requires the factoring method as a prerequisite.

edges.successor_name: Quadratic Formula to Solve Quadratic Equation
edges.rationale: Students typically learn factoring first; the quadratic formula is introduced for cases factoring cannot handle.

edges.successor_name: Write Linear Equation from Slope and y-intercept
edges.rationale: Writing y = mx + b from points requires computing slope from points first.

edges.prereq_name: Write Linear Equation from Slope and y-intercept
edges.rationale: Graphing y = mx + b presupposes being able to write such an equation.
Percents are fractions with denominator 100; 'percent of' generalises 'fraction of'.
